Fact: Blastoise shares its category with Kabuto and Kabutops. They are all known as the Shellfish Pokémon.
Despite this, Blastoise is based on a turtle not a shellfish. This may be due to the fact the Japanese category is more accurately translated "Shell" not "Shellfish".
Early concept art for a Pokémon called "Karabajio" resembles Blastoise without its signature water cannons. The name may be a combination of "carapace" and Caravaggio.
Mega Blastoise is the only Mega-Evolved first partner Pokémon that does not have two types.
The way Blastoise flies in New Pokémon Snap is a potential reference to Gamera. Pokkén Tournament DX and Pokémon Scarlet and Violet have also introduced similar motions for Blastoise's actions.
Blastoise was originally considered for Super Smash Bros. Brawl alongside Charizard and Ivysaur, but director Masahiro Sakurai decided to go with Squirtle instead for evolutionary stage balance.

In 3D games before Pokémon X and Y, when Blastoise uses Hydro Pump, two streams are fired from its cannons, whereas every other Pokémon only has one stream. Starting in Pokémon X and Y, Blastoise no longer had a unique animation for this move. However, the animation was brought back when Blastoise returned in The Indigo Disk expansion of Pokémon Scarlet and Violet.
